programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Big Trouble in Little China
programme.desc: Truck driver Jack Burton gets embroiled in a supernatural battle when his best friend Wang Chi's green-eyed fiancée is kidnapped by henchmen of the sorcerer Lo Pan, who must marry a girl with green eyes in order to return to the human realm.

programme.title: Nobody
programme.desc: Hutch Mansell, a suburban dad, overlooked husband, nothing neighbor — a "nobody." When two thieves break into his home one night, Hutch's unknown long-simmering rage is ignited and propels him on a brutal path that will uncover dark secrets he fought to leave behind.

programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do America
programme.desc: Slacker duo Beavis and Butt-Head wake to discover their TV has been stolen. Their search for a new one takes them on a clueless adventure across America, during which they manage to accidentally become America's most wanted.

programme.title: Friday
programme.desc: A lot can happen between Thursday and Saturday - Before the sun sets, Craig must get his friend out of trouble, find a new job so his parents don't evict him, escape his girlfriend long enough to capture a new woman's heart and survive a climatic confrontation with the neighborhood menace.

programme.title: My Blue Heaven
programme.desc: An all too uptight FBI agent must protect a larger than life mobster with a heart of gold, currently under witness protection in the suburbs.

programme.title: Police Academy 5: Assignment: Miami Beach
programme.desc: Eric Lassard is about to retire, which Harris plans to exploit to take over his position. In Miami, where Lassard is to be honored, a mix-up leads to an adventure involving diamonds, kidnapping and betrayal.

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
programme.desc: As a swinging fashion photographer by day and a groovy British superagent by night, Austin Powers is the '60s' most shagadelic spy. But can he stop megalomaniac Dr. Evil after the bald villain freezes himself and unthaws in the '90s? With the help of sexy sidekick Vanessa Kensington, he just might.

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
programme.desc: Kevin is separated from his family again when he accidentally boards a flight to New York City during a Christmas trip to Miami. However he crosses paths with the same burglars, who now plan to rob a toy store on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: At an exclusive country club, an ambitious young caddy, Danny Noonan, eagerly pursues a caddy scholarship in hopes of attending college and, in turn, avoiding a job at the lumber yard. In order to succeed, he must first win the favour of the elitist Judge Smails, and then the caddy golf tournament which Smails sponsors.

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite
programme.desc: Preston, Idaho's most curious resident, Napoleon Dynamite, lives with his grandma and his 32-year-old brother (who cruises chat rooms for ladies) and works to help his best friend, Pedro, snatch the Student Body President title from mean teen Summer Wheatley.

programme.title: Police Academy 6: City Under Siege
programme.desc: Our favourite police men are called together to deal with a gang who rob banks and jewelers. Using their various talents as well as their extraordinary luck, the crooks stand no chance against our men and women in blue.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds
programme.desc: At Adams College, the jocks rule the school from their house on high, the Alpha Beta fraternity. So when a group of socially-challenged misfits try to go Greek, they're instantly rejected by every house on campus. Deciding to start their own fraternity to protect their outcast brothers, the campus nerds soon find themselves in a battle royale as the Alpha Betas try to crush their new rivals.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors
programme.desc: A clumsy young man working at an impoverished flower shop discovers that the strange plant he has been nurturing has an insatiable appetite for blood, forcing him to kill to feed it.

programme.title: The Monkey
programme.desc: When twin brothers Bill and Hal find their father's old monkey toy in the attic, a series of gruesome deaths start. The siblings decide to throw the toy away and move on with their lives, growing apart over the years.

programme.title: The Monolith Monsters
programme.desc: Rocks from a meteor which grow when in contact with water threaten a sleepy Southwestern desert community.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian priest named Imhotep is revived when a British archaeological expedition finds his mummy and one of the researchers accidentally reads an ancient life-giving spell. Imhotep escapes from the field site and searches for the reincarnation of the soul of his lover.

programme.title: The Mummy's Curse
programme.desc: An irrigation project in the rural bayous of Louisiana unearths living mummy Kharis, who was buried in quicksand twenty-five years earlier.

programme.title: The Mummy's Ghost
programme.desc: An Egyptian high priest travels to America to reclaim the bodies of ancient Egyptian princess Ananka and her living guardian mummy Kharis. Learning that Ananka's spirit has been reincarnated into another body, he kidnaps a young woman of Egyptian descent with a mysterious resemblance to the princess. However, the high priest's greedy desires cause him to lose control of the mummy.

programme.title: The Mummy's Hand
programme.desc: A couple of comical, out-of-work archaeologists (Dick Foran and Wallace Ford) in Egypt discover evidence of the burial place of the ancient Egyptian princess Ananka. After receiving funding from an eccentric magician (Cecil Kellaway) and his beautiful daughter (Peggy Moran), they set out into the desert only to be terrorized by a sinister high priest (George Zucco) and the living mummy Kharis (Tom Tyler) who are the guardians of Ananka^Òs tomb.

programme.title: The Mummy's Tomb
programme.desc: A high priest travels to America with a living mummy to kill those who had desecrated the tomb of an Egyptian princess thirty years earlier.

programme.title: The People Under the Stairs
programme.desc: When Fool breaks into the home of his family's landlords, he discovers that they have mutilated several boys and kept them imprisoned under stairs. As Fool attempts to flee, he meets their daughter, Alice. Can Fool and Alice escape?

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: When a downtrodden janitor, Winston Gooze, is exposed to a catastrophic toxic accident, he’s transformed into a new kind of hero: The Toxic Avenger. Now, Toxie must rise from outcast to savior, taking on ruthless corporate overlords and corrupt forces who threaten his son, his friends, and his community.

programme.title: The Wolf Man
programme.desc: Upon his return to his father's estate, aristocrat Larry Talbot meets a beautiful woman, attends a mystical carnival and uncovers a horrifying curse.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Aerosmith Live Osaka, Japan 1999 Millennium Countdown 2000
Paul McCartney Back In The US
Lennon Legend - The Very Best Of John Lennon
Led Zeppelin Live Royal Albert Hall
The Who - Live At The Isle Of Wight Festival (1970)
ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The girls' dream vacation in the Caribbean turns out to be far from what was advertised in the brochure. While the girls are away, Sophia romances the gardener.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose suspects Miles has been unfaithful to her when his name is found in the hotel registry dating back several years.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred gets a new next door neighbor and is shocked to learn that he is Puerto Rican and planning on opening a junk business of his own. Fred gets upset about the competition and contrives a plan to get the city to condemn his property especially since, Julio has a pet goat.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith leaves Archie when she finds out he dated flirtatious waitress while she was volunteering at the Sunshine Home.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith forgives Archie's indiscretions after Mike and Gloria bring the two together for an emotional reunion.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Episode #137 - And estranged Archie and Edith are determined to prove to each other that they are perfectly content going their separate ways, in the conclusion of a three-part presentation. The going gets tough for a while when the Bunkers continue to resist Mike and Gloria's efforts to reconcile them.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Heather & Tina vs. Margie & Trish vs. Willie & Ray

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Holly & Mindy vs. Eileen & Yvette vs. Tracy & Tim
programme.desc: Clips from this episode were later seen on VH1's "I Love the '90s: Part Deux"

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Shemp is a sick man with a bad case of nerves. The stooge's friend Claude, a self-taught healer, tries to cure Shemp with various home- made remedies. When nothing seems to work, Claude suggests they buy his old lemon of a car so they can take Shemp on a trip to the country. The car won't start, and the trip never gets off the ground, but not to worry, Shemp is cured by all the excitement.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are private detectives looking for a missing millionaire. They wander around the millionaire's spooky mansion confronting various crooks and a dangerous dame.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in ancient Egypt, the stooges run a used chariot lot where they unload defective chariots on unsuspecting customers. When they gyp the head of the palace guard, they're brought to the palace to be executed, but instead become royal chamberlains after curing the King's toothache. When they recover some tax money stolen by a corrupt official, the King rewards them with marriage to his daughter. After getting a look at the ugly crone, Moe and Larry select Shemp to be the groom.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges reminisce about the girls they met overseas while in the military. As they wait for the girls' ship to arrive, they get drunk and Shemp winds up asleep with his feet in a tub of cement. After sobering up, they free Shemp with a dynamite blast that lands them at the dock where their sweethearts are waiting.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Larry and Shemp reminisce about their experiences with Jean, a diamond crazy gold digger each of them was gypped by. After telling their stories, they have a run in with Moe, who is now married to the same women. When Jean shows up, they deliver some stooge-style revenge.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges decide to run a fish business, and catch their own fish, but the whole thing turns to disaster when they're mistaken for Japanese spies when their boat sinks, and they raise a paint-splattered rag to signal for help.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Moe is on trial for assaulting Curly and Larry with an ax. Moe relates how Curly and Larry took him on a hunting trip for his nerves. Out in the woods they confronted a bear which Curly and Larry stunned, and thinking it was dead, threw it in the back of their car, where it came awake, tossed Moe out and drove the car into a tree. The judge finds Moe not guilty and Moe promptly goes after Larry and Curly again with the ax.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Nancy introduces her new girlfriend Sharon to Roseanne and Jackie. Sharon invites them to go dancing with her and Nancy at a gay bar and winds up giving Roseanne a romantic kiss. This freaks Roseanne out, but she has a hard time figuring out why. Jackie is openly uncomfortable about people assuming she might be gay, which prompts an amused Roseanne to behave like they are a couple.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie tells Roseanne that Fred wants to be in the delivery room, and the hospital will only allow one extra person to be with her. When Jackie realizes that she is in labor, Roseanne puts off calling Fred to inform him, hoping she'll get to be the one who stays with Jackie, but she feels guilty later and sends Dan out to find him. Bev reveals to Jackie that her birth name was "Marjorie," but little Roseanne's version, "My Jackie," stuck.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Audrey cries to Dan about his father owing her money, so he confronts Ed, hoping he will do the right thing. Jackie and Fred go out on a "get-to-know-you-better" date to try to build an honest, solid relationship, but Jackie's admission that she's had many more sexual partners than he has causes a big step backward in their fragile relationship. D.J. goes through a rebellious stage.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne helps Dan understand his dad's lying about his mom's mental state.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: I Pray the Lord My Stove to Keep
programme.desc: Darlene discovers that D.J. has been sneaking off after to school to attend church; D.J. gives Roseanne a hard time about morals when she accepts a mistaken delivery of a second stove at the diner.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Bev moves in after breaking her pelvis at the retirement home, blaming Dan for not safeproofing her shower; then Roseanne discovers that it happened while Bev and Jake were having sex.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Fred asks Roseanne how to propose to Jackie, then uses hints from Jackie's favourite soap opera; an enraged Roseanne demands romance from Dan, then turns to fantasy; Mark and David struggle with the concepts of feminism.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Egos clash when Jackie and Fred can't make a decision about their marriage. Fred is having what Dan calls the moment of clarity, a case of the jitters that affects some men just before the big day. But what's really bothering Fred is his belief that Jackie has feelings for someone she sees almost every day and whom she is always asking to come over and fix stuff, none other than Dan.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mrs. Slocombe temporarily takes the place of Mr. Rumbold, who has taken ill . She eats the cream cakes she finds in his office. But she then gets a call from the hospital warning that the ice cream is what made him ill.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr. Franco of the sports department reveals that Captain Peacock has an enormous boil on his buttocks, leading to the staff making jokes at his expense. In defense of his honor, Peacock challenges Franco to a boxing match.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony scramble to find a third person to appear in their Christmas card photo.
Featured cartoons: Weasel While You Work (1958) The Peachy Cobbler (1950) Cracked Quack (1952) A Street Cat Named Sylvester (1953) Freeze Frame (1979)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An art critic, Dale Kingston, kills his uncle for his valuable collection of paintings. All the clues lead to the dead man's first wife. Can Columbo correct the Lieutenant's error before it is too late?

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude is stunned to discover a black man picketing in front of her home. It isn't until later that she discovers that Walter and her have unwittingly become the owners of a rundown apartment building and they are now being labeled ""slumlords"".

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter and Maude go to Massachusetts for an appliance seller's convention. When their reservations at a upscale hotel are botched, they must share a shabby motel. This is when a squabble ensues when Maude tries to confide in Walter the angst she has over not having any meaning in her life and the troubles she's been having trying to find a job because she's a woman.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Laid off from work, Dorothy searches for part-time work which leads to Blanche getting her a job at the museum. Then fur flies when Dorothy is handed the job of organizing a banquet, the duty Blanche was hoping for. Meanwhile, Rose finds a stray dog at the supermarket.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose places an ad in the personals column and she is let down when she doesn't hear an immediate response. This leads Blanche and Dorothy to begin responding under the name Isaac Newton.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host an extraordinary lineup of exciting battles, including a Career-Threatening Match between Greg "The Hammer" Valentine and "Rugged" Ronnie Garvin and "Ravishing" Rick Rude taking on Hercules. Plus, Jake "The Snake" Roberts, The Rockers, and many more in action.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Linda worries that her relationship with Louise isn't as good as it should be, so, much to Louise's chagrin, she forces the young lady to participate in a mother-daughter seminar. With mom away, Tina turns to Bob for a lesson in shaving her legs.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Robert gets hurt by a bull while on duty, Ray starts feeling tremendous guilt after Robert's partner reveals that he was only on duty because Ray cancelled their plans, so that he could race go-karts.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Still recuperating from his recent run-in with a bull, a cantankerous Robert is driving everyone crazy with his mean-spirited behaviour. When his doctor tells him that he needs two more weeks of of recovery, which means two more weeks at his parents' house, his attitude worsens. But Debra things that there is another reason for Robert's bad attitude.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug feels the urge for a new car and against Carrie's advice buys it for himself only to find out moments later that the workers at the IPS company are going on strike. He unsuccessfully tries to return the car, forcing Carrie to work major overtime to compensate for his mistake.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: It is week two of the IPS strike, and Doug still has not gotten a job. To get back on his feet, his sister Stephanie get him a substitute teacher job at her school. However, it is a high school, and the kids are not so nice. Can Doug survive?

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: When Lloyd Braun comes back from the psychiatric hospital, Kramer takes him under his wing and convinces Jerry to buy gum from him and wear glasses that don't belong to him to prove that Lloyd isn't crazy. George tries to prove to his old neighbor Deena that he isn't crazy after a cashier short-changes him.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: After George's parents take back the bread they gifted to Susan's parents, George makes Jerry find another loaf and tries to appease Susan's parents by taking them on Kramer's Manhattan horse tours, to disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: I Pray the Lord My Stove to Keep
programme.desc: Darlene discovers that D.J. has been sneaking off after to school to attend church; D.J. gives Roseanne a hard time about morals when she accepts a mistaken delivery of a second stove at the diner.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Bev moves in after breaking her pelvis at the retirement home, blaming Dan for not safeproofing her shower; then Roseanne discovers that it happened while Bev and Jake were having sex.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: When Dwight embarrasses Michael by punching him in the stomach at work, Jim organizes a lunchtime rematch at Dwight's Dojo. Meanwhile, Pam becomes annoyed at Jim when he gets too physical with her, Ryan updates the emergency contact information on a secret mission, and Michael tantalizes Ryan when the boss gets a hold of Ryan's cell phone number.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: At a Chili's dinner with a potential client, Michael's antics, Jan's professionalism, and an impromptu "Baby Back Ribs" duet unexpectedly close the deal, while Dwight and Kevin read Jim's top-secret document.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty announces his retirement due to interference from network executives and the growing popularity of the big-money game show "Me Wantee", but when Krusty reveals to Sideshow Bob that all of the episodes featuring him have been erased, Sideshow Bob uses Krusty's biggest fan (Bart) to murder him during Krusty's farewell show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart, Nelson, Millhouse, and Ralph are recruited by a man named L.T. Smash to be in a boy band called the Party Posse but when Lisa discovers the true identity of LT. Smash as a navy recruitment officer she tries to tell Homer and Marge with hilarious results.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a Good Samaritan after discovering the simple joys of helping people in need–which is put to the test when he goes on a hunger strike after the owner of the Springfield Isotopes attempts to discredit him when Homer stumbles on his plot to discreetly move the team to Albuquerque, New Mexico.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Lisa becomes the target of a female bully, Lisa discovers a scientific reason as to why bullies pick on nerds. Meanwhile, Homer goes into the baby-proofing business, which is threatening the businesses of pediatricians and greeting card salesmen.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When the Simpsons run out of food thanks to a bag boy strike (caused by Homer), the family finds an old box of animal crackers with a sweepstakes that sends The Simpsons to Africa.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundys are going to a family wedding for Al's cousin. But Peggy can't decide what to wear, so while she dresses (and redresses) Al waits and daydreams. Meanwhile, Kelly color coordinates with two fellow bridesmaids, deciding that tight black leather is always best, and Bud gets to know the young bride to be much better than he should!

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: It Doesn't Get Any Better Than This
programme.desc: It's no vacation for Al when Peggy and Marcy join him on his winter fishing trip and make him referee to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Kelly, Bud, and Seven, left behind with no food or money, help Jefferson spend all of Marcy's money.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Working at the diner is getting to Kelly, so she decides to rebel by getting a motorcycle which Peggy deems as too dangerous, until Peggy tries it for herself and gets some good vibrations. But it's Al who really takes to the new bike and imagines himself as an "easy rider,"... at least until an untimely head-on meeting with the Dodge.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy decides to take up a hobby by drawing Al and in so doing, creates her own magazine cartoon of Al makes him famous: the hapless shoe salesman titled "Mr. Empty Pants". Al would prefer the fame to be fleeting, until another magazine (Playgirl) declares him to be a sex symbol.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After one too many dateless Saturdays, Bud appears on a new TV dating game show called "You Can't Miss" for the less experienced, but not quite with the expected results. Meanwhile, the low IQ Kelly believes that there's a conspiracy to keep her from knowing what day it is.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy tells Seven a fantasy store about pirates and beautiful princesses.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al competes in an athletic championship for seniors.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After Al knocks out and captures a burglar that breaks into his house one night while he's sleeping on the couch after an argument with Peggy, he becomes a neighborhood hero. But the tables soon turn when the burglar sues Al for injuring him and the Bundys must go to court once again.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: I Dream of Choo Choo Rabinowitz
programme.desc: Lamont's mother-in-law to be is coming to Watts for a visit and Fred is a little concerned about what she's going to think of a common junk man. This leads Fred to decide to create a legacy and tries to set a world's record and get his name in the Guinness Book of World Records. His goal is to be the person to stay awake the longest.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred becomes a victim of a con game when, two men convince him to begin a bogus special "Sanford & Son" sales promotion, in which the lucky winner wins $500. However, Fred has no intention of handing out that much bread but unknown to Fred, the con men have rigged the game so they end up being the lucky winners and Fred will have to pay them the money after all.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred has been chosen to represent the Watt's businessmen on a community relatives committee and his first official decision is to allow the public library to be open an hour longer. However, his next decision doesn't seem to be so easy to decide after a crooked businessman tries to bribe Fred with $1000 to vote for a building expansion.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont threatens once again to leave the junk business on account that they aren't making much money. However, he also wants to quit and get a full-time job due to his impending nuptials. Fred then hatches a scheme by borrowing money from the bank and pretending that business is picking up big time, while secretly working as a waiter at a restaurant down town.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.sub-title: The Prettiest Week of My Life
programme.desc: Chris decides to enter the exciting world of male modeling when he joins the Handsome Boy School of Modeling.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris convinces his father to accompany him to the father-son competition at the annual newspaper boy picnic.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck is desperate to elude the draft board respresentative bearing his conscription order.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: When Elmer Fudd disturbs Bugs with his railroad surveying, Bugs fights back.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc:
Yosemite Sam means to hold up the Super-Chief train and Bugs is out to stop him.
Note: First appearance of Yosemite Sam, whose prototype, Red Hot Ryder, opposed Bugs eight months earlier in Buckaroo Bugs (1944).

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Everything seems to be going all right when Bugs starts working in a department store. That is, until he gets involved with taxidermy.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Beaky Buzzard and his bothers are sent out by their Mother to bring home something to eat.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs Bunny tricks Elmer Fudd into believing his house has been quarantined for something called "rabbititus.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A pet duckling grows up to be Daffy. His antics soon grate on the family, culminating in a fencing match at the breakfast table fought with butter knives. But the daughter defends Daffy.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: After midnight in a closed bookstore all the books come to life.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Highland High evacuates after receiving a bomb threat. Beavis & Butt-head stay, try to find the bomb, and cause some destruction of their own.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The guys follow VanDriessen and his date home, hoping to see them "doing it".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After being congratulated for thwarting an attempted robbery at Burger World, Beavis and Butt-head start ""arresting"" everyone in sight.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut divides the class into boy/girl pairs, and assigns each a ""sugar baby"" which they must care for and nurture for one week. Beavis and Butt-head are paired together (as each girl in the class has refused to be assigned to them), and promptly give their "baby" a bath -- in a stream.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After the TV goes out, the boys sleep through four disasters that ravage their town.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: At Burger World, Butt-head realizes that he gets paid when he goes to the bathroom.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Tom Anderson takes Beavis and Butt-Head to the woods to hunt deer.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
The boys visit a drawing class (in hopes of getting free nachos) and see nude models.
videos:
Nine Inch Nails - "March Of The Pigs"
Dick Dale - "Nitro"
Superchunk - "Package Thief"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get trapped in an elevator with an attractive woman and do all they can to escape.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys win the lottery and buy a riding lawn mower.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Aaaaugh! The secret word is "zyzzybalubah," and that can only mean one thing! The entire Playhouse, including Reba, is sucked into space, transported to a strange planet, and taken over by an odd, lonely alien! Yvona, an alien from the planet, is trying to escape the unfriendly Zyzzybalubah, but Pee-wee teaches him how to be polite and make friends. Reba teaches Pee-wee how to whistle, and she sings a song.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Guess who's having a pajama-party sleepover? That's right, it's Pee-wee along with his co-hostess, "the most beautiful woman in Puppetland," Miss Yvonne! Jambi's got a head for pajama fashion when he gives Cowboy Curtis a Wild-West pair of P.J.s! Wacky Roger, the Monster, keeps an eye on things as Pee-wee decides that, since he loves his fruit salad so much, he's going to marry it! "Watch" for the secret word!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are invited to be extras on a popular TV show shooting a scene in Han’s diner, but when Caroline catches the director’s eye, he offers her an actual role – with strings attached.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline’s father asks her to go on a talk show to defend him when a former employee threatens to defame him in a tell-all book.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another exciting edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring an incredible lineup of in-ring confrontations. The Barbarian goes one-on-one with Hercules. King Haku takes on Big John Studd, and much more. Plus, the anticipation for the upcoming release of No Holds Barred continues to build.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: With few options left, Rhaenyra summons a trusted knight for a risky venture and plots with Mysaria to catalyze the smallfolk. Meanwhile, Daemon's unease grows, and Aemond takes steps to reshape the Green Council.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: What Is Dead May Never Die
programme.desc: Tyrion tries to see who he can trust in the Small Council. Catelyn visits Renly to try and persuade him to join Robb in the war. Theon must decide if his loyalties lie with his own family or with Robb.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Lord Baelish arrives at Renly's camp just before he faces off against Stannis. Daenerys and her company are welcomed into the city of Qarth. Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie find themselves imprisoned at Harrenhal.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A 6-year-old girl suffers ailments expected in patients much older. Tensions between Chase and Cameron lead House to intentionally assign them to the same tasks, including investigating the young girl's home.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The doctors try to find what's wrong with a scam artist who collapses after her brain "freezes," but the case becomes personal for Foreman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A midlife crisis prompts Homer to quit his job and become an inventor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After accidentally killing a nesting bird, Bart looks after her eggs, but when they hatch the babies are not fledgelings but tree lizards, who deposit their own eggs in birds' nests. They are quite unattractive but Bart takes to them, giving them names. They do, however, come into their own when it comes to ridding Springfield of its pigeon problem.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In "Hell Toupée," Snake is sent to the electric chair, and his hair is transplanted onto Homer. In "The Terror of Tiny Toon," Bart and Lisa become cartoon characters in an episode of Itchy & Scratchy. In "Starship Poopers," a growth spurt casts doubt on Maggie's paternity, and the family goes on the Jerry Springer show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: When You Dish Upon a Star
programme.desc: After a chance encounter with Hollywood celebrities secretly living in Springfield, Homer becomes their friend and gofer... until he spills the beans to the gang at Moe's.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Needing to know his middle name for form-filling purposes, Homer visits Seth and Munchie,two ageing hippies who knew his mother,at their farm and decides he too wants to be a hippy, Unfortunately he succeeds in ruining their organic juice-making apparatus and,to atone,makes juice of his own,using the hippies' supply of personal vegetables. This leads to Homer hallucinating and a run-in with the police.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Stuck at home with a cold, Lisa gets addicted to a video game and neglects her homework. Her A+++ on a test wins the school a grant, but when she confesses that she cheated, Skinner covers it up. Meanwhile, Homer raises a lobster.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Grampa needs a kidney transplant Homer is the obvious donor but he chickens out and runs away to sea. On board ship his fellow passengers are disgusted by his cowardice and throw him overboard. Drifting to the shore he sees a father and son bond over building a sand-castle and knows what he must do. The transplant is a success - though its circumstances are unusual,to say the least.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a riot at a sci-fi convention, Mayor Quimby hires Homer as his bodyguard, and Homer witnesses the rampant corruption in town politics. When Homer takes a stand, he runs afoul of the Springfield mafia.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is astonished to learn that Ned is sixty years old and,unusually, Ned admits that he wished he had done some more reckless things in his life so Homer takes him to Las Vegas. Here they both get extremely drunk and wake up to find that they have married cocktail waitresses Amber and Ginger. Eventually the two men are thrown out of Las Vegas and hitch a ride back to Springfield,Homer getting attacked by a vulture.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After drunks vandalize the school the children are blamed and subject to a curfew. Having seen a creepy 'Village of the Damned'-type movie 'The Bloodening' Bart leads the kids in a revolt against their elders,frightening them into believing the children have supernatural powers. But then the senior citizens arrive and they reckon that everybody under the age of 70 should be made subject to a curfew.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: To ensure that Ken Shamrock, Mankind, and The Rock don't team up with "Stone Cold" Steve Austin against The Undertaker and Kane, Mr. McMahon places all three men in a Triple Threat Match to determine the No. 1 Contender for the WWE Championship. The Women's Championship is reintroduced as Sable and Jacqueline face off to bring home the gold.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira meet with a key group of people, Alexis struggles with her new job, David offer's emotional support to his boss' daughter.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira have to deal with a surprise motel guest while Alexis and David go apartment hunting.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to prove their cleaning skills to Sophie so she will hire them for her maid service.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie's (Jennifer Coolidge) beauty overwhelms Earl, Caroline and Max wind up spending Valentine's Day at the hospital; Caroline tries to rekindle an old flame.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have to make kosher cupcakes when they get a gig at a bar mitzvah.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend their spring break dog-sitting at a couple's lavish apartment.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max convinces Caroline to partake in a clinical drug trial in an effort to raise money to help Caroline’s father.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After promising Earl to drop his tax return in the mail, Caroline learns that Max has never filed, and gets sidetracked trying to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The stakes are high when a socialite Manhattan mother hires Max and Caroline to serve cupcakes at her daughter's first birthday party.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And Martha Stewart Have a Ball (1)
programme.desc: Max and Caroline plan to attend a big fashion gala at the Metropolitan Museum of Art where Martha Stewart is scheduled to be a guest, in an attempt to have her taste their cupcakes and get her seal of approval.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline show their true colors when the diner gets robbed. Meanwhile, everyone sees a surprising new side of Han.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to take their cupcake business to the next level by auditioning for the reality cooking show Cupcake Wars.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have different ideas on how to handle their new pre-approved credit card.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: The Greyhounds try a new strategy that has everyone thinking outside the box. Sam prepares to host a VIP guest at Ola's.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: While watching Henry, Ted fights the urge to spiral when Michelle and Dr. Jacob go on a romantic trip. An online leak has massive implications for Keeley.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A rumor involving his cousin puts Tony's relationship with Johnny Sack back on thin ice. Meadow helps get her boyfriend Finn a job. A.J. ponders a 'maximum-security summer' at Casa Carmela. Melfi links Tony's current guilt to a past job. Carmela learns that good legal help is hard to find.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony B. and Christopher unearth some old memories during an otherwise idyllic trip to the country. Tony and Carmela plan another party. Janice lands in anger-management therapy after a sports-rage incident. Benny and Terry solve a missing-Vespa mystery. Tony puts his sister's newfound serenity to the test.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's resolve is put to the test. Meanwhile, Jason wrestles with his feelings and Chidi surprises Eleanor.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael arranges an important meeting and Janet makes a reconnection.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Rhaenyra looks to gain an advantage by unusual means, Daemon pressures a young liege lord to raise up his bannermen.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons are surprised to discover that their past actions have consequences.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin's voices may be breaking, but they're showing no signs of growing up. Whilst Gumball and Darwin make fun of Mom and Dad for being old, their voices start to break. They try to ignore it, but everybody they meet reacts to their new and unpredictable voices.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The new coach at Elmore High stands up to Jamie. The new Coach witnesses Jamie bullying the other kids at lunch and steps in. Jamie is made to apologize, much to the amazement of the other kids. Gumball and Darwin worry about Jamie seeking revenge, especially when they see her sneaking into the back of Coach's car.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Watterson children's long-awaited pet puppy turns out to be a turtle which hates them.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Mom reveals that Gumball's real name is "Zach," Gumball adopts a cheesy, annoying persona to match the name. His family tries to make him remember the old Gumball, as this new Zach persona is progressively taking over Gumball's life and erasing his memories.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: What happens to the Titans after TEEN TITANS GO! To the Movies.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have Raven give them new super powers, but have a hard time adjusting.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Aqualad uses shrimps and prime rib to try to win back Raven.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans want to be the scariest trick-or-treaters in town, so Raven casts a spell to turn them into real monsters.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries again to tell the real origin story of the Teen Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin takes the other Titans on a trip through the dizzying world of quantum physics.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries to teach the other Titans about real estate, but they’re only interested in finding a villain to fight.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin takes the Titans on an educational trip to the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: How's This for a Special? Spaaaace (1)
programme.desc: After receiving a galactic crime alert, the Titans take off for an epic space adventure. The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme, but the excitement of the space adventure might be too much for Robin to handle.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy, starving, exploit the benefits of living as babies. Then, George Liquor American takes us the Dog Show, where Ren & Stimpy are pitted against some tough competition. But only George can handle the judge's harsh scrutiny.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy squeezes, and a stench is born. Ren thinks it's just a stinky fantasy, but when Stinky floats away, Stimpy abandons his friend to search through rain and snow and dark of night. It'll take a Christmas miracle for Stinky to return to his warm, snugly home!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy want the easy life of living in a zoo. They disguise themselves as monkeys and try to hang in there.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren shows his soft side when he volunteers to be a fake dad for a 200-pound prison convict named Kowalski. Of course, Stimpy lends support as the loving fake mom.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy go camping and learn how to rough it in nature. And what could be rougher than skinny dipping with Stimpy? Ren's about to find out.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: While eating a chili dog, Beavis sniffs a piece of onion and begins to tear up, but Butt-Head mistakes it for crying and mocks Beavis relentlessly.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Six Million Dollar Man Rusts to Bits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross tries to make money by collecting his family allowances. He also tries to adopt Christine, who is using an exercise bike and some special effects to travel around the world.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hit and Run on Sesame Street will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Despite her constant attempts to explain safety rules, bad things keep happening to Christine including electric shock and decapitation.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Dukes of Hazard Dress Up in Drag will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The rest of the cast is upset that Christine, as host, has an easier job. They trade places with her and all co-host the show, while she takes their positions in the sketches. In the end, she ends up on a quest to get Brodie slimed.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Jaws Eats Jacques Cousteau will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine hosts this episode from her bathtub at home. During the show she gets surprise visits from the cast and crew. The episode ends with her getting sucked down the drain.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Atomic Blast Levels Fantasy Island will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Jami admits that the only reason the Indians smoked was to kill the white settlers. He then tries to convince Christine to smoke.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers Vandalizes the Neighborhood will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The entire set is stolen piece by piece, including the microphones and Christine's clothes.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers, Neighborhood Pusher will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast uses pies as a substitute for pot to demonstrate the perils of drug use.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Miss Piggy Eats Ham Sandwiches will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine explains the importance of a healthy diet, but ends up wearing more of the food then she eats.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle rages between those who help people get hitched and those who hitch trailers. Also, Vic and Kenny get a visit from some truckers, and Ken practices his future work as a Justice of the Peace.
Games include: Mudd Butlers; Tour de Grand Prix; Brass Balls; Nut Baggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
MXC goes under the lights for the first nighttime edition as Wall Street's Bulls and Bears challenge Bourbon Street's distillers and beer makers. Also, Vic preaches to Kenny about the dangers of alcohol.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Eat Shitake; Pole Riders; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It's a monstrous edition of the world's toughest reality sports show in town as product mascots, captained by Pissin' Boots, the mascot of Cat-Scat Kitty Litter challenges real monsters, captained by Clamula, whose reeking breath kills people for miles around as we celebrate All Hallow's Eve.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Intestinal Fortitude; Dry Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle would be settled between the aerospace workers and the fast food employees in MXC's Arctic Edition.
Games include: Sno' Man's Land; Frozen Wallbangers; Sperm Wheelers; Frigid Slope of Icy Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Republicans vs. Democrats vs. Third Party
programme.desc:
MXC presents their first ever three-way... battle in honor of the Presidential electoral college entitled "Squeeze Out The Vote 2004" where it's the Republicans against the Democrats against the Third Party. Forget the backstabbing and name-calling because this time, the mud-slinging's for real!
Games include: Rock the Moat; Dark Horse Race; Pork the Barrel; The Rotating Surfboard of Political Suicide; Mudslingers; Log Jam

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The people who can't hold a job are facing off against the tree-huggers who want to save the earth when the unemployed challenge the environmentalists in this MXC battle.
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Finger It; Dope on a Rope; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Snack Food Industry vs. Print Media
programme.desc:
If you're hungry for action, this episode of MXC is just for you. Members of the snack food industry challenge their arch rivals from the print media in a winner-takes-all battle for bragging rights.. .and a cold soda or two. Also, the Captain introduces everyone to his new book, "Well, You're Wrong!".
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Door Jam; Circle Jerkers; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Baby product makers challenge the people who track down UFOs and ghosts on this edition of MXC in a war to settle their score, and nap time afterward for everyone.
Games include: Mud Butlers; Yankin' It; Flailing Wall; Log Drop

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Grateful for her help in art class, dimwitted cheerleader Brittany Taylor invites Daria to her popular-crowd party in a gated community. Though uninterested, Daria realizes that attending the party would be a perfect opportunity to humiliate Quinn. She takes Jane along for kicks...hopefully.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Fearing that Daria and Quinn won't be prepared for college, after meeting some clients who are preparing their three-year-old daughter for it, Helen and Jake force them into Lawndale High's college prep course, and take them on a tour of their alma mater; Middleton College. Like so many parents, they find college life has changed since they've been there, and both girls get caught up in two different activities while getting the guided tour.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale's local cybercafé has a break-in, and Mr. O'Neill thinks he can prevent future vandalism and theft by restoring it as one of the hip coffee houses of the 1960's. When Daria is forced into joining the other students into raising money for repairs, and later performing on stage with one of her essays, the kids, parents, and teachers of Lawndale have no idea what she has in store for them.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Mrs. Bennett takes her economics class on a field trip to the Mall of the Millenium. Daria and Jane want nothing to do with this trip, but have no way out of it, however all is not lost when Daria discovers Quinn and the rest of the Fashion Club skipping school there.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High has a couple of visitors, claiming to be modeling scouts looking for the next Christy Turlington. Quinn and Brittany have high hopes. Daria and Jane are revolted by their presence.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Kevin are forced to work as lab-partners in science class. The homework consists of teaching a mouse tricks. Daria does all the work, of course, while Kevin watches football on TV and flirts with Quinn.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets Daria to babysit for her. Daria, who has no interest in being an authority figure since she doesn't respect authority, is stuck with two well-behaved "PC" kids.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn decides she needs cosmetic surgery, and drags Daria to the doctor with her. She is happy to consult with Quinn, but very interested in Daria, whom she views as a long term project and potential masterpiece.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Professor Crumhorn creates a robot duplicate of Penfold (which is far more efficient than the original). At the push of a button it transforms into a giant machine intent on destroying Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Mark III runs out of gas, leaving DM and Penfold stranded in the Sahara Desert.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: While on holiday in Spain, Penfold is kidnapped by a mad Coyote who thinks he is Don Quixote. They go tilting at a windmill that happens to contain Baron Greenback's latest device for world domination.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: DM and Penfold are taking a holiday in New York provided by the nice folks at the FBI. Doctor Augustus P. Crumhorn III takes the opportunity to get his revenge on Danger Mouse by swallowing his transformation pills and posing as a terminally cute young girl who claims her father has been kidnapped.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Just when Penfold should be meeting his Aunt and the airport, he and Danger Mouse are sent to the Amazon jungle where a tribe have been sacrificing people to The Great Ant God from Ataxia. Penfold is captured, and things look grim, but then Penfold's Aunt arrives from a taxi and tells them all to stop playing silly games.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: There's A Penfold In My Suit
programme.desc: Penfold puts on Danger Mouse's suit and can't get out. From then on everyone thinks he is Danger Mouse. Later, DM and Penfold go to Bratislavakia to find out why all the countries of Central Europe have swapped places. There they fall fowl of a mystical stone that causes people to swap bodies.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn ""Ka-zonks"" DM and Penfold into a hallucinatory world in an attempt to drive them crazy.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Flashpaper - Broom Trick - Panama Canal - Hero's Voice - Nipple Expansion - Exercise For Cows - Reaction Time - Solar Mirrors - Tablecloth Pull

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Hot Air Balloon - NYC To Tokyo - Whispering Tube - Hero's Hand - Robots In The Factory - Optical Illusions - I-Beam

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Dancing Spaghetti - Centipede - Dribble Glass - Joining Paper Clips - Hibernation - Hero Counts - What Do You Hear? - Pitcher Plant - Drop Balls & Card

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explores the functions of the human mouth, and what causes bad breath. Phoebe has fun with worms. Gulliver solves the mysteries of the visual scale.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains what constitutes a catalyst and how an aerosol can operates.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the characteristics of rubber, how flowers bloom and uncovers the mysteries of hair growth.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reviews Bactrian and dromedary camels, covers the densities of gases and explores the Dead Sea.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discovers boomerangs, visits the soil, finds bacteria and the dung beetle then examines the mysteries of the circus.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: About 70% of the Earth’s surface is covered in water, and almost all of that water has been around since the Earth formed billions and billions of years ago. That means a glass of water you drink today could be water that a dinosaur once sipped. Water is constantly recycled on Earth as rain, snow, sleet and hail. It makes its way in and out of oceans, lakes, streams, hail, and glaciers. Scientists call the recycling of water the water cycle (not that bad, huh?).

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Friction is a force that slows moving things down and turns the moving energy into heat energy. When two things rub together, like your bike tires and the road, friction between them slows you down. There’s also friction in the metal parts of the wheel’s hub – at the center. There’s even friction between the fibers and rubber of the tires themselves as they flex and roll. That’s why you eventually stop rolling when you stop pedaling. Rough things make more friction than smooth things. Rubber shoes on a clean wooden basketball floor create more friction than do hard metal skate blades on smooth ice.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball and Darwin begin to complain about every little thing in their lives, the people of Elmore City mistakenly assumes charity cases and put together a benefit to raise money for them, leaving them having to fix the mix-up.
